title: Upper Face Injection techniques | Botox & Filler Periorbital Rejuvenation Tutorial
filename: Upper Face Injection techniques ｜ Botox & Filler Periorbital Rejuvenation Tutorial.en.vtt
excerpt: This week I'm sharing a procedure that's all based around peri-orbital Rejuvenation how do you make a patient look fresher younger and happier by treating this area we're going to cover the lateral logic Junction a small toxin eyebrow lift and a cheek treatment that are all desig…

segments.text: this week I'm sharing a procedure that's all based around peri-orbital Rejuvenation how do you make a patient look fresher younger and happier by treating this area we're going to cover the lateral logic Junction a small toxin eyebrow lift and a cheek treatment that are all designed to support the eye and make it as it should be the most beautiful part of the face [Music] so first step this is a little brow lift we want to treat orbicularis oculi we don't want to treat the frontalis muscle because that would cause the opposite a drop so if you could just close your eyes tightly for me squeeze tight you can see that activity here we can relax and this is not specifically to treat lines but we're going to improve them as well so I'm going to Mark out four injection points

and this will just cause a little lift in the eyebrow but you will never get the Spock brow from this kind of treatment it's much gentler than that technique very important to be superficial we have a very thin layer of dermis two millimeters just pop the needle just under that and it then sits on top of the muscle or just one unit is all we need for a brow lift and we do those four injections we're sitting just on top of orbicularis oculi the orbit is then shielded by the muscle and also the membrane that Shields the orbit so the chance of a ptosis is very low if you inject very superficially so I can see a little vein here that's always worth checking before you inject you can easily avoid a bruise if we just avoid that

so in terms of treatment design now I'm going back to what I'm trying to achieve in terms of the aesthetic I want the angle the most projected part of the cheek to be here it's natural as you get older for it to move anterior and inferior so it's probably about here if I can add a little bit more curvature here focusing the cheek that's going to be very beautifying I then need to have that shape blend in laterally and then blend it into the lower the cheek Junction so angle of the cheek I find it easier to palpate this so feeling for the angle is better than drawing lines on the face and trying to predict where it is so that's always my first step and I can feel the greatest projection of the zygomers on this line often parallel with electrocanthus then you can use this line here between the root of the Helix and the Ayla base there's a line that tracks around and

that usually crosses at the same point so that is the point I need to be more projected now once I've done that I know that I'm going to have to also add volume here and then this the sooth muscle which is slightly depleted if I can add volume there it'll create roundness in the whole cheek area and then we'll blend that back with a couple more injections laterally most likely depending on how that goes now I don't I personally prefer having a plan but then the plan changes as you execute because you'll see things evolve and I might do something slightly different but I will have a good reason for each difference each variation I take off this Clan it's worth just pointing out there's some natural asymmetry this cheek is

Fuller and there's more less work will be required to harmonize it this one has a little bit more of an indentation so that it becomes more apparent as we start to inject I usually start with projecting projection points so the point at which the face is defined you have the GoNoodle angle go no angle here sorry the chin the nose the projection of the brow and the cheek those are your key angles in the face and I'm going to start by projecting out and then blend those in so I'm looking for a periosteal level injection pull back slightly it might give me a bit more lift place that in touch the bone aspirate give it a second and then I can start injecting looking for a response from her of discomfort which might make me pause and

find another spot that all looks good don't point to come out slowly and then a little shape I can now see that projection of the zygoma that I wanted to create I know that her cheek moves like this and so the next point will be lateral to that where there's usually a weak point in the skin so I'm going to put my next injection just there touching periosteum that's great so now I have definition of the zygoma but you can see it has highlighted the issue we've already pointed out which is the lateral logic Junction so

I'll use a different product to harmonize that in a moment let's do a more lateral injection point just to harmonize this and hopefully give a little bit more lift a little search for the zygoma is normal next part of this treatment is the lateral logic Junction I'm feeling for the zygomatic facial foramen which is only present in 30 of people but as sometimes associated with a small artery so I want to make sure I preferably can't feel that I do feel does that feel different to other parts of your skin a bit more sensitive a bit more tender

sometimes if the where the artery is there's a nerve and it can feel different not entirely sure it doesn't feel most obvious for amen I've got one myself so I can actually feel it on my own face um but I'm hopeful we don't only present in 30 people but I will if you touch it with a needle it does obviously hurt because you've got a nerve there so hopefully it'll feel normal and that's a key point to support you can see there's a line that actually runs right across that so if I can support that area it's going to support that line and also improve the curvature of the whole cheek so we're through orbicularis oculi into the cheek fat it's great give it a second and then a little though let's you should be able to see that looking

less cluttered already subtracted that shadow if you open your eyes for us again yeah so I'm also just looking at this part of the lateral logic Junction so more immediately and I think I can make an improvement here if we can just place a little bit of product on the periostim there very similar injection just edging down shouldn't be deep you go too deep you have probably angled your needle to go into the cheek and we'll do a little bit less a little smooth over now you should be able to see that cheek is now much more harmonized already with flowing easily from one surface to the next instead of having those little bumps and disruptions so the next thing I want to do is we've now got a very defined cheek but it's almost a little too defined if you

compare with the other side of her face it's which obviously is untreated but I think we need a little bit more roundness in this cheek here to prevent it being too defined make it fit in so once you're off the zygoma where if you think about the anatomy here we're on top of the zygomaticus major zygomaticus minor and underneath just over here is the infraorbital artery so we want to be a bit more superficial this is in the fat rather than on the periosteum and once you're off that part of the zygoma to using voluma intermediate depth now I'm looking at when the needle's in how it changes the shape that is the shape I want to see a little bit more roundness aspirate 0.5 come back a little like an aspirate again nothing to stop the aspirating twice another little bolus

and then we smooth it in smooth it don't move it as I say so once again feel for the angle of the zygoma looking for that line of projection pull back and touch the periosteum aspirate so just like the other side there's a little fracture line you can often see here that's where my second injection goes on the line between the Ayla base and the helix

I really like that side but it was the best side already so I'm using it for inspiration to try and get this side to match so I'm going to do a little additional bit of volume laterally so what I'm seeing on this side that I like is nice cheek projection up here and this side is still a little bit flatter so I'm going to use a slightly higher Geo Prime G Prime product and just try and add a little bit of that elevation and this is what Richard De Mayo calls the Top Model look it's just an elevated cheekbone might as well go through the same hole again if I can avoid bruise right [Music]
